name: Zimbabwe

colors.symbolism: the blood shed during the war of liberation
the heritage and ethnicity of the native African people

description: Seven equal horizontal stripes — green, yellow, red, black, red, yellow, green — overlaid by a white isoceles triangle at the hoist (extending two sevenths of the way across the flag). On the triangle is a red five-pointed star surmounted by a yellow stylised Zimbabwe Bird.
history: Adopted on 18 April 1980, the day Southern Rhodesia became the independent Republic of Zimbabwe. The colours are those of the ruling Zimbabwe African National Union – Patriotic Front (ZANU-PF), the party that led the liberation struggle. The Zimbabwe Bird is a depiction of an ancient soapstone sculpture found in the ruins of Great Zimbabwe (11th–15th century).

features.symbolism: a palindromic seven-stripe pattern based on the colours of the Zimbabwe African National Union (ZANU-PF), the party that led the independence movement; the colours are also the Pan-African colours
the nation's aspirations, hope for the future, and internationalism (a socialist symbol)
the national emblem of Zimbabwe — a stylised stone-carved bird from the medieval ruins of Great Zimbabwe; represents the historical heritage of the nation
